evidence_spans.text: arXiv:1605.09223 states that the Croot-Lev-Pach method bounds progression-free subsets of F_q^n by c^n with c < q; for q = 3 this is the cap-set problem.

assertion.text: Bloom-Sisask break the 'log barrier' for Roth: density alpha > 1 / (log N)^{1+c} for an absolute c > 0 suffices for a 3-AP, the first sub-1/log N bound.

assertion.text: Ruzsa triangle inequality: for any finite subsets X, Y, Z of an abelian group, |X-Z| * |Y| <= |X-Y| * |Z-Y|. A foundational sumset inequality underpinning Plunnecke-Ruzsa and the Freiman-Ruzsa theorem.

conditions.text: Finite subsets of an abelian group; triangle-style inequality on sumset cardinalities.

assertion.text: Cilleruelo 2010: B_2[g] sets in {1,...,N} (sets where each integer has at most g representations as a sum of two elements) have cardinality at most sqrt(g*N) + O(N^(1/4)), generalizing the Lindstrom-Erdos-Turan bound from g=1 to g>=2.

conditions.text: B_2[g] sets in {1,...,N} for g >= 1; upper bound on cardinality scaling as sqrt(g*N).
